experiences.intentions: Active J piece is near the top in spawn orientation over a mostly jagged stack. The immediate goal is to place it without creating extra overhangs while setting up future T/L/T/I pieces. There is a vertical gap around column 4-5 near the top-middle and some unevenness below; rotating right to orientation 1 makes the J vertical with the nub to the top-right, which is more flexible for tucking into central wells than the flat spawn shape. Left/right moves first are possible later, but improving orientation now preserves options and avoids early commitment. Hard/soft drop now in current orientation would likely create a worse shelf. So rotate_right is the best setup action.
